goal: Close Rust enum + C++ pure-virtual abstractness gaps left open by Intent B

summary.title: Close Rust enum + C++ pure-virtual abstractness gaps
summary.what: Adds Scope.ENUM_ITEM to the AST registry; registers it in Rust.classes(); Rust.is_abstract_scope classifies enums as concrete (sum types are concrete data definitions). Implements Cpp.is_abstract_scope by walking the class_specifier body for field_declaration nodes carrying both a function_declarator AND a direct ``number_literal`` ``0`` child — tree-sitter-cpp's emission for the ``= 0`` pure-virtual idiom. C++ structs classify as concrete unconditionally. Updated existing Rust test to assert nc=2; added 3 C++ tests covering pure-virtual abstract, plain-virtual concrete, and struct concrete. Full suite 535/535.
summary.why: Intent B documented two scope limits: Rust enums uncounted and C++ classes unclassified (all C++ packages would land in 'unknown' zone). The user flagged both as needing closure before moving to the deps_kernel retirement intent — C++ has inheritance, and Rust enums are a first-class type, so leaving them out of the packages metric would silently under-represent abstractness for those grammars. Closing the gap here keeps the redesign's per-language coverage honest before layering further work on top.
summary.user_goal: Close Rust enum + C++ pure-virtual abstractness gaps left open by Intent B

decisions.chose: Rust enums classify as concrete, not as 'uncountable'
decisions.rationale: Rust enums are sum types: their variants are data, not virtual methods. They have no abstract members and can be instantiated directly. Mapping them to concrete (Nc) matches both the legacy kernel's behaviour and the Martin model semantics — they're a definite-shape data type.

decisions.chose: C++ pure-virtual detection by walking class_specifier body field for field_declaration + function_declarator + number_literal
decisions.rationale: Tree-sitter-cpp emits ``virtual T foo() = 0;`` as a field_declaration with both a ``function_declarator`` and a ``number_literal`` ``0`` as DIRECT children. Concrete methods (``T foo() { return 0; }``) emit as function_definition (different node type) and any ``0`` inside the body sits deeper. Checking only direct children of field_declaration cleanly discriminates. No need to inspect the ``virtual`` keyword separately — the ``= 0`` form requires virtual, so the literal-zero direct child is sufficient.

decisions.chose: C++ struct classifies as concrete regardless of pure-virtual content
decisions.rationale: C++ structs are technically classes (only differ in default member access), so pure-virtual is syntactically legal. But the abstract-class IDIOM uses ``class`` by convention; treating struct as always-concrete avoids false positives for plain data-holding structs and matches the legacy kernel.

review_notes: Rust's classes() now includes enum_item — this means CK metrics (WMC/CBO/DIT/NOC) also see enums as class scopes. Enums have zero methods, so WMC=0. CBO walks identifier refs; enum variants are mostly self-references and built-in types, so CBO impact is minimal. DIT requires superclasses; enums have none. NOC counts subclasses; enums have none. Verified by full suite (535/535) — no CK test regressions.

C++ test coverage is intentionally narrow (3 cases). Edge cases not covered: classes with only ``= default``/``= delete`` (those use the ``default``/``delete`` keywords, not ``number_literal``, so they correctly don't trigger the pure-virtual path); template classes (template_declaration wraps class_specifier; the body walk descends through it correctly since we use child_by_field_name('body')).

architectural_claims: Rust grammar's classification surface now matches the language's actual type taxonomy: struct + enum (concrete data), trait (abstract contract).
C++ abstractness signal is now substrate-native — no legacy kernel reference. Pure-virtual detection lives in Cpp.is_abstract_scope via direct AST walk.
behavioral_changes: Rust packages with enums now contribute to Nc instead of being uncounted.
C++ packages with class definitions now classify into pain/uselessness/warning/clean/ok zones instead of universally landing in 'unknown'.
Rust's classes() set widens by one entry (enum_item); CK metrics for Rust enums become WMC=0 / DIT=0 / NOC=0 (verified zero regressions).
